goal: Create a versioned AI UI protocol foundation so CopilotKit/AG-UI and a native AG-UI-compatible UI can be developed in parallel on the same backend contracts.
global_constraints: Django remains the system of record for sessions, permissions, history, audit and confirmation.
Agent runtime must not depend on CopilotKit-specific concepts.
Native UI must support AG-UI-compatible events before adding local extensions.
Local project extensions must be versioned and carried through CUSTOM or namespaced STATE_DELTA events.
Browser clients must ignore unknown extensions safely.
Frontend UI commands must never perform domain writes directly.
Do not expose Django AI gateway tokens, cookies, raw actor context, raw PII, UNC paths or full DOM in protocol state.
Keep /chat and /chat/stream compatible until explicit owner acceptance.
Keep the legacy sidebar as fallback.
Update .desc.json and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
cross_cutting_acceptance: ADR-0028 captures protocol versioning and driver boundaries.
A neutral Django UI runtime module owns actor signing and driver selection.
services.agent_runtime.protocols contains common internal events and AG-UI adapter modules.
open_right_panel command is versioned and normalized in one shared place.
CopilotKit works through the common foundation without user-visible regression.
Native UI can use the AG-UI-compatible endpoint without Copilot Runtime.
Legacy UI remains available as a configured fallback.
E2E covers legacy, CopilotKit and native driver smoke scenarios.
implementation_notes: Land the shared foundation in main before widening native UI work.
Prefer additive protocol changes and capability negotiation over breaking event shapes.
Keep CopilotKit package-specific code inside the CopilotKit driver only.
Make the native UI a direct AG-UI client first; add a native protocol adapter only if a concrete gap appears.
Duplicate localBusinessUiCommands temporarily only to preserve the existing CopilotKit island.
